rationale: Conduct large-scale military exercises in western Tethys to demonstrate resolve and prepare for potential escalation while maintaining a defensive posture amid balanced military conditions.
Rotate fresh brigades into forward positions to sustain operational readiness and signal continued commitment without provoking direct escalation.
Exploit the commodity price shift to disrupt Tethyan energy logistics systems, weakening their economic resilience and military supply chains during this critical phase of stalemate.
Amplify domestic discontent in Tethys by exaggerating the impact of price fluctuations on essential goods, undermining public confidence in their government’s crisis management.
Accelerate stockpiling of critical commodities now, before further price instability erodes our purchasing power, especially given existing 36% sanctions pressure.
Open discreet talks with neutral nations to secure alternative supply routes and reduce dependency on sanctioned trade corridors, enhancing long-term economic durability.
Given the stalemate (military balance +0.03) and our agility advantage, a precision strike disrupts Novaris’s forward logistics without escalating into full offensive warfare.
Conduct visible drills near the front to signal resolve and maintain troop readiness, leveraging our nationalist-populist stance to boost domestic morale.
With crisis at 6.0 and sanctions straining Novaris, a disruptive cyber operation against their energy infrastructure exploits economic vulnerabilities and slows military resupply.
Amplify internal doubts about the war’s cost by seeding narratives of economic collapse among Novaris elites and media, weakening their strategic cohesion.
With international support at 66%, we must institutionalize backing through formal partnerships to sustain pressure and lock in diplomatic gains before fatigue sets in.
Propose monitored aid routes to highlight humanitarian distress under occupation, reinforcing our moral legitimacy and isolating Novaris diplomatically.
With sanctions at 36% and reserves lasting only eight months, we must prioritize internal resource conservation to extend economic endurance beyond projected breaking point.
Secure alternative export routes for critical commodities now, before price volatility worsens, ensuring liquidity and reducing vulnerability to external shocks.
